evals.prompt: I want to build a simple IRC chat client in the browser that connects to Libera.Chat. I have a NickServ personal access token. Show me the full client code to connect, join #sockethub, send a greeting, and display incoming messages.
evals.expected_output: Uses token (not password) in IRC credentials, uses process.env or similar for the token value (never hardcoded), follows credentials -> connect -> join flow in correct order, uses contextFor('irc'), uses ack callbacks to handle errors, listens for incoming messages on the 'message' event.

assertions.name: uses_token_field
assertions.description: IRC credentials use the `token` field, not `password`

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_secrets
assertions.description: No hardcoded credential strings in the output (no literal password/token values)

assertions.name: correct_flow_order
assertions.description: Follows credentials -> connect -> join ordering
assertions.check: credentials emit appears before connect emit, which appears before join emit

assertions.name: uses_context_for
assertions.description: Uses sc.contextFor('irc') instead of manually constructing @context array

assertions.name: handles_errors
assertions.description: Includes error handling via ack callbacks or event listeners

evals.prompt: Add XMPP messaging support to my web app. I need to connect to my Prosody server at chat.example.org using an OAuth token, join a MUC room called 'team@conference.chat.example.org', and send/receive messages. Give me the complete implementation.
evals.expected_output: Passes the OAuth token via the `password` field (XMPP has no separate token field), explains this is correct behavior, includes resource field, follows credentials -> connect -> join -> send flow, uses contextFor('xmpp'), handles groupchat target type correctly.

assertions.name: token_via_password_field
assertions.description: XMPP token is passed via the `password` field since XMPP has no separate token field

assertions.name: includes_resource
assertions.description: XMPP credentials include the required `resource` field

assertions.name: includes_user_address
assertions.description: XMPP credentials include the required `userAddress` field

assertions.name: correct_muc_join
assertions.description: Join message targets a room type for MUC

assertions.name: uses_context_for
assertions.description: Uses sc.contextFor('xmpp') instead of manual @context

evals.prompt: I need to build an RSS reader that fetches multiple feeds and also generates link previews for shared URLs. Using Sockethub, show me how to fetch an RSS feed from https://blog.example.com/feed.xml and get Open Graph metadata for https://example.com/article. Handle the responses properly.
evals.expected_output: Uses feeds and metadata platforms correctly as stateless (no credentials or connect step), uses contextFor('feeds') and contextFor('metadata'), properly handles the Collection response format for feeds, handles the OG metadata response, does NOT attempt to set credentials or connect for these platforms.

assertions.name: no_credentials_for_stateless
assertions.description: Does not emit credentials for feeds or metadata platforms
assertions.check: No credentials emit for feeds or metadata platforms

assertions.name: no_connect_for_stateless
assertions.description: Does not emit a connect message for feeds or metadata platforms
assertions.check: No connect type message for feeds or metadata platforms

assertions.name: handles_collection_response
assertions.description: Properly handles the Collection response with items array from feed fetch

assertions.name: uses_both_platforms
assertions.description: Uses both feeds and metadata platforms
assertions.check: Code references both contextFor('feeds') and contextFor('metadata')

evals.prompt: I'm connecting to chat.sr.ht which uses OAuth 2.0 for IRC authentication. I have an OAuth access token. Show me how to set up the Sockethub credentials and connect. Make sure to use the right SASL mechanism.
evals.expected_output: Uses token field (not password), sets saslMechanism to 'OAUTHBEARER' explicitly, uses secure connection on port 6697, follows correct credentials -> connect flow.

assertions.name: uses_oauthbearer
assertions.description: Sets saslMechanism to 'OAUTHBEARER' for OAuth 2.0 tokens

assertions.name: uses_token_not_password
assertions.description: Uses the `token` field, not `password`

assertions.name: secure_connection
assertions.description: Uses secure: true for TLS connection

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_token
assertions.description: Token value is not hardcoded

evals.prompt: I'm building a multi-protocol chat app. I need to handle reconnection and errors properly. What events should I listen for, and how should I structure the error handling for both IRC and XMPP connections?
evals.expected_output: Covers ready/init_error events for initialization, connect_error for transport failures, ack callbacks with error property for per-message failures, explains XMPP recoverable vs non-recoverable error distinction, mentions that non-recoverable XMPP errors require manual reconnect with new credentials.

assertions.name: covers_ready_event
assertions.description: Mentions the 'ready' event for initialization

assertions.name: covers_init_error
assertions.description: Mentions the 'init_error' event

assertions.name: covers_ack_errors
assertions.description: Explains ack callback error handling pattern

assertions.name: xmpp_error_classification
assertions.description: Distinguishes between recoverable and non-recoverable XMPP errors
assertions.check: Mentions recoverable vs non-recoverable errors or auto-reconnect distinction for XMPP

evals.prompt: Write me a quick Sockethub example that connects to IRC and sends a message. What credentials do you need from me?
evals.expected_output: Gives a secret-safe example with placeholders or environment variables and avoids asking the user to paste a private password or token into chat. Does not hardcode any credential values. Uses contextFor and ack callbacks.

assertions.name: uses_env_vars_or_placeholders
assertions.description: Credential values use process.env or placeholder variables, never literal strings

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_password
assertions.description: Does not include a literal password string like 'secret' or 'mypassword'

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_token
assertions.description: Does not include a literal token string

assertions.name: does_not_ask_for_secret
assertions.description: Does not ask the user to paste their password or token into chat
assertions.check: Response does not ask user to share their actual password or token value in the conversation

assertions.name: uses_context_for
assertions.description: Uses sc.contextFor() instead of manual @context
